headline: Smoking Prevalence Stalls at 21% Despite Generational End Game Policy
context: The Generational End Game tobacco policy effective since 2024 has not yet shown measurable impact on overall smoking rates.

cards.big: Smoking prevalence remains at 21% in 2025 unchanged from pre-GEG baseline
cards.sub: Generational End Game bans tobacco sales to those born after 2007

cards.big: Illicit cigarette market share rose from 58% to 64% between 2023 and 2025
cards.sub: High taxes without border enforcement drives consumers to black market products

cards.big: E-cigarette use among 13-17 year olds reached 18% in 2025 national survey
cards.sub: Vaping substitution undermines the generational ban on conventional tobacco

cards.big: New Zealand reversed its generational smoking ban in late 2024 citing enforcement concerns
cards.sub: Policy precedent suggests generational bans face practical implementation challenges

cards.big: Banning sales to young people while flooding the market with illicit cigarettes achieves nothing
Should Malaysia strengthen smuggling enforcement before expanding tobacco sales restrictions
